metadata.title: GoLife Admin
metadata.description: Operational surface for GoLife AI mission quality and system health.

shell.summary: This panel watches the same product loop as mobile: capture, risks, missions, feedback, trust.

nav.title: GoLife Admin
nav.subtitle: A field board for mission quality, trust, support, and AI cost.

items.title: Missions
items.note: Ranked output quality and risk links.

items.title: Feedback
items.note: Why users accept, reject, or complete.

shared.privateNoteRedacted: Private note kept out of admin telemetry.
shared.legacyPrivateNoteRedacted: Legacy private note redacted from admin view.

dashboard.title: Operational pulse for the personal OS
dashboard.description: Watch whether GoLife is generating useful missions, staying inside trust boundaries, and keeping AI cost under control.
dashboard.usefulMissionsNote: North star of real product utility.
dashboard.aiCostNote: Keep the margin visible while usage grows.
dashboard.safetyRateNote: Signals whether prompts are drifting into regulated or unsafe territory.
dashboard.activeKeysNote: Healthy keys currently available to the OpenRouter executor.
dashboard.disabledKeysNote: Paused or invalid keys excluded from live routing.
dashboard.snapshotAgeNote: Older snapshots increase the chance of using stale model rankings.
dashboard.briefingNote: The board is organized around the same three questions product asks every morning: are missions useful, are risks clear, and is trust intact?
dashboard.flagsNote: Operational rollouts stay visible here so mobile, gateway, and admin work from the same switchboard.

aiCosts.title: Provider cost and endpoint pressure
aiCosts.description: The fastest way to lose margin is to ignore which endpoint is expensive, slow, or frequently falling back. This board keeps that visible.
aiCosts.totalCostNote: Estimated spend across ranked missions, classification, and support endpoints.
aiCosts.trackedEndpointsNote: Operational breakdown by endpoint rather than by provider alone.
aiCosts.averageFallbackNote: High fallback can mean prompt trouble, timeout pressure, or quota issues.
aiCosts.panelNote: Classify cheaply, rank carefully, and keep feedback storage nearly free.

feedback.title: Feedback journal
feedback.description: Accepted, completed, and rejected missions are the live teaching signal for the ranking layer.
feedback.eventsNote: Stored signals available to improve future ranking.
feedback.positiveNote: Positive signals that should reinforce similar mission patterns.
feedback.rejectedNote: The clearest early warning for repetition or poor context.
feedback.panelNote: Admin keeps status and domain metadata for learning. Private note text stays out of this surface.

users.title: User state and support signals
users.description: A compact list for account health, current plan mix, and who may need export, delete, or trust follow-up.
users.weeklyActiveNote: Users who are still inside the weekly mission loop.
users.supportQueueNote: Export or delete requests that need manual action.
users.privacyRequestsNote: Open export/delete requests visible without raw personal data.
users.panelNote: The detail page combines account profile, usage footprint, mission history, and feedback journal.
users.filterQueryPlaceholder: Search by user, masked email, or display name
users.emptyBody: Adjust the search, plan, status, or locale filters and try again.
users.detailEmptyBody: Pick a row to inspect usage, privacy, and support metadata.
users.drawerSensitiveExcluded: Sensitive content is excluded from this operator surface.

userDetail.description: This dossier combines current plan, live usage footprint, mission outcomes, and the feedback that should shape ranking next.
userDetail.aiCallsNote: Total operational gateway traffic so far.
userDetail.usefulMissionsNote: Completed or accepted missions that mattered.
userDetail.fallbackRateNote: How often this user needed deterministic fallback.
userDetail.accountNote: Support flags are early warning markers for trust, monetization, or product learning.
userDetail.behaviorNote: This page keeps only safe usage, privacy, and support metadata in the operator view.
userDetail.emptyBehavior: No mission or feedback records found for this user yet.
userDetail.metadataOnlyLabel: Sensitive content is excluded from this admin view.

featureFlags.title: Feature flags
featureFlags.description: Flags keep mobile, AI gateway, and admin aligned on which behavior is live, which is in shadow mode, and which stays behind operator control.
featureFlags.panelNote: Read fallback values if the backend is offline. When the admin API is live, each toggle sends a real PATCH request.

usage.title: Capture and mission throughput
usage.description: A per-user view of how often the product is used, how often fallback is needed, and where latency is drifting.
usage.captureEventsNote: Signals whether capture is frictionless enough to become habit.
usage.missionsGeneratedNote: How many ranked plans the system has emitted.
usage.averageLatencyNote: A direct operator check on AI responsiveness.
usage.panelNote: This is the operational view to compare capture depth, fallback, and mission execution user by user.

safety.title: Safety incidents and blocked behavior
safety.description: Operators need a compact trace of blocked outputs, regulated advice attempts, and actions that required stronger confirmation.
safety.highSeverityNote: Anything high needs immediate operator review.
safety.financeIncidentsNote: Financial advice pressure remains the most sensitive regulated area.
safety.panelNote: Each event preserves who triggered it, which rule fired, and the domain that needs closer prompt or policy work.

missions.title: Ranked outputs and linked risks
missions.description: This table is the quality gate for whether the system is creating actions worth doing, not just cards worth rendering.
missions.trackedNote: Current audit slice from the operational backend.
missions.completedNote: The strongest proof of practical utility.
missions.rejectedNote: A direct signal for repetitive or low-context recommendations.
missions.panelNote: Each row keeps domains, matched risks, usefulness, and final score in one place.
missions.empty: No mission records available in this slice yet.

modelCatalog.title: OpenRouter model catalog
modelCatalog.description: This cache is the raw material for routing decisions. Operators should check freshness, context windows, and structured-output capability before trusting a snapshot.
modelCatalog.panelNote: The control plane filters these models before ranking them per capability.
modelCatalog.empty: No cached models are available right now.

openrouterKeys.title: OpenRouter keys and failover health
openrouterKeys.description: Track which keys are active, which ones are degraded, and whether GoLife is rotating safely when a route or quota fails.
openrouterKeys.activeKeysNote: Healthy keys available to the gateway control plane.
openrouterKeys.disabledKeysNote: Keys excluded from routing because they are paused or unsafe.
openrouterKeys.recentEventsNote: Latest success and failure records written by the gateway.
openrouterKeys.keyPoolNote: Secrets never render here. Operators only see masked labels, health, and rotation order.
openrouterKeys.rotationLogNote: This is the first place to look when a capability starts falling back too often.

routingProfiles.title: Capability profiles
routingProfiles.description: Each capability keeps its own latency, throughput, parameter, and context rules so the gateway does not route everything with one blunt policy.
routingProfiles.panelNote: These rules gate the whole OpenRouter catalog before ranking. Keep them strict enough to protect structured outputs.

routingSnapshots.title: Current top-3 model selections
routingSnapshots.description: This page shows the ranked output of the control plane after catalog filtering, telemetry weighting, and policy checks.
routingSnapshots.capabilitiesNote: Each enabled capability should expose exactly three candidates.
routingSnapshots.snapshotAgeNote: Older snapshots increase the chance of routing stale models.
routingSnapshots.selectedRowsNote: Total ranked rows stored across all capabilities.
routingSnapshots.panelNote: The gateway asks OpenRouter for these three models in this order, with provider fallback enabled.
routingSnapshots.empty: No ranked selections are available right now.

supportQueue.title: Export and delete operations
supportQueue.description: Privacy trust is operational, not theoretical. This queue keeps export and delete requests visible and actionable.
supportQueue.exportNote: Users asking for their portable data bundle.
supportQueue.deleteNote: Users requesting full account or data removal.
supportQueue.panelNote: The web backend currently exposes a seeded queue. Replace this with persistent operational data in the next backend block.

modelSettings.title: Provider and model settings
modelSettings.description: This page keeps the main operational model choices visible so cost, fallback, and classification strategy stay explicit.
modelSettings.panelNote: Classification should stay cheap and deterministic when possible. Higher-cost reasoning belongs in ranked synthesis or weekly review.
